response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, seasoned engineer with roughly 18 years of experience, strong testing craftsmanship, and some genuinely high-scale work (low-latency services handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic). However, the role is a Principal IC6 position specifically scoped to growth and experimentation systems, and the resume shows no experience with experimentation platforms, A/B testing, feature flighting, telemetry/metrics infrastructure, or statistical analysis. His career trajectory is solidly senior-level product and platform work rather than principal-level, multi-workstream architectural leadership across teams at enterprise scale. The fundamentals are real, but the core domain gap is too large for this particular req.
strengths: 18+ years of broad engineering experience across web, backend, embedded, and mobile, meeting the language and tenure bar (TypeScript/JavaScript, Java, C#, C/C++)
Proven high-scale distributed systems work: low-latency, async-I/O services handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Strong quality engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, authored test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), plus recent AI-agent/MCP tooling experience
concerns: No experimentation, A/B testing, feature-flagging, or growth-engineering experience — the core domain of the role
No demonstrated telemetry/metrics platform ownership or statistical/experimental analysis background
Career history is senior-IC scoped; little evidence of principal-level, cross-team architectural leadership or multi-workstream initiatives at enterprise scale

key_factors.factor: Strong engineering fundamentals and high-scale systems experience
